← All Digests Tuesday, March 17, 2026

NVIDIA Launches AI Agent Platform at GTC, OpenAI Courts PE Giants, Anthropic Commits $100M to Enterprise

Today's top stories in AI + Product Marketing

1

NVIDIA Launches Enterprise AI Agent Platform — Adobe, Salesforce, SAP Among 17 Adopters

At GTC 2026, NVIDIA unveiled a comprehensive enterprise AI agent platform. Adobe's Shantanu Narayen announced they're integrating Firefly models, CUDA libraries, 3D digital twins for marketing, and Agent Toolkit with Nemotron for "enterprise-grade AI workflows of the future." Salesforce and SAP are also among the 17 launch partners. When the three biggest enterprise software players align on one agentic AI platform, PMM teams need to pay attention.

Read on VentureBeat →
2

OpenAI Courts Private Equity for Enterprise AI Joint Venture

Reuters reports OpenAI is in advanced talks with TPG, Advent International, Bain Capital, and Brookfield to form a joint venture distributing enterprise AI across their portfolio companies. This would give OpenAI instant reach into thousands of businesses — and put GPT-based tools on the fast track to becoming standard enterprise infrastructure.

Read on Reuters →
3

Anthropic Launches Claude Partner Network with $100M Commitment

Anthropic is doubling down on "corporate governance" positioning. The new Claude Partner Network commits $100 million to support enterprises adopting Claude with transparency and technical support. While OpenAI chases scale through PE, Anthropic is betting that trust and governance will win the enterprise AI race.

Read on devFlokers →
4

AI Is Repricing the Marketing Stack, Not Collapsing It

Smart take from MarTech: AI isn't killing MarTech tools — it's making coordination tools easier to replicate, putting pricing pressure on parts of the stack that deliver "convenience rather than operational risk." Translation: point solutions that just save time are in trouble. Tools that reduce risk or unlock differentiation will command premiums.

Read on MarTech →
5

Forrester: 88% of B2B Organizations Adopting or Planning AI Agents

Forrester's latest State of Customer Obsession Survey confirms what we're seeing: 88% of B2B organizations are adopting or planning to adopt AI agents. The adoption intent is there — but the gap between intent and execution remains the biggest opportunity for PMM leaders who can operationalize these tools.

Read on Forrester →

💡 My Take

Today's headline: NVIDIA at GTC. When Adobe, Salesforce, and SAP — three of the biggest names in enterprise software — all commit to the same AI agent platform on the same day, that's not a partnership announcement. That's the infrastructure layer of enterprise AI being locked in. PMM teams should be asking: "How will AI agents built on this platform change how our buyers research, evaluate, and buy?"
